metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 13/12/2024), Rwanda Social Security Board document library
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (President; countersigned by the Prime Minister)
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Social Security Board library print of the Official Gazette no Special of 13/12/2024 carrying Presidential Order No 086/01 of 12/12/2024 fixing the contribution rate for the mandatory pension scheme (6-page text-embedded trilingual print), issued under Article 8 of Law No 05/2015. Grounds Article One: the rate of contribution under the mandatory pension scheme is fixed at 12 percent of the remuneration subject to contribution from 1 January 2025, then 14 percent from 1 January 2027, 16 percent from 2028, 18 percent from 2029 and 20 percent from 2030; Article 5 repeals Presidential Order No 009/01 of 10/05/2016 (the prior 6 percent total); Article 6 entry into force on publication. Under the equal employer/employee sharing rule of Law No 05/2015, the 12 percent total is the 6 + 6 percent split RWAMOD RW_2025 applies to formal employment income.
metadata.title: Presidential Order No 086/01 of 12/12/2024 fixing the contribution rate for the mandatory pension scheme - 12 percent from 1 January 2025 with the stepped ladder to 20 percent by 2030

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda; Rwanda Legal Information Institute (Laws.Africa) AKN source facsimile
metadata.source_note: Rwanda Legal Information Institute (rwandalii.org, Laws.Africa AKN mirror) source PDF of Prime Minister's Order No 105/03 of 30/09/2020 related to the community-based health insurance scheme contributions (7-page text-embedded print; mirror host recorded; gazetted 01/10/2020), issued under Law No 03/2015. Grounds Article 5: the contribution of an employee in a State organ and of an employee in the private sector is zero-point five percent (0.5%) of the net salary of the employee, withheld by the employer and deposited to the CBHI scheme account, with monthly declaration and payment; also carries the scheme's funding-source schedule (Article 3) including the Ubudehe category E State-paid premiums of the pre-2026 framework. In force throughout CY2025; repealed with effect from 23/02/2026 by Prime Minister's Order No 001/03 of 16/02/2026 (captured alongside). RWAMOD RW_2025 applies the 0.5 percent levy on net salary for formal employees (tsceehl03).
metadata.title: Prime Minister's Order No 105/03 of 30/09/2020 related to the community-based health insurance scheme contributions - employee contribution 0.5 percent of net salary

Grounds Article 5: the contribution of an employee in a State organ and of an employee in the private sector is zero-point five percent (0.5%) of the net salary of the employee, withheld by the employer and deposited to the CBHI scheme account, with monthly declaration and payment; also carries the scheme's funding-source schedule (Article 3) including the Ubudehe category E State-paid premiums of the pre-2026 framework. metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no Special of 23/02/2026, Year 65); gazettes.africa archive facsimile (rwandalii.org AKN entry point redirects here)
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Prime Minister; Minister of Finance and Economic Planning)
metadata.source_note: Official Gazette no Special of 23/02/2026 (Year 65; 25-page text-embedded trilingual print served by the gazettes.africa archive facsimile, to which the Rwanda Legal Information Institute AKN entry point redirects; host recorded) carrying two CBHI instruments issued under Law No 03/2015: Prime Minister's Order No 001/03 of 16/02/2026 on funding and contributions (Article 17 repeals Prime Minister's Order No 105/03 of 30/09/2020; entry into force on publication) and Ministerial Order No 002/26/10/TC of 16/02/2026 determining the amount of contribution and modalities for payment (Article One: a member registered at IMIBEREHO Dynamic Social Registry level one pays FRW 4,000 per person per year paid by the State; level two pays FRW 3,000 plus FRW 1,000 by the State; level three FRW 5,000; level four FRW 8,000; level five FRW 20,000; Article 5 entry into force on publication 23/02/2026). This is the first gazetted carrier of the informal-sector premium schedule that RWAMOD RW_2025 already applies by monthly-income tier (3,000 to 30,000 FRW; 5,000 to 60,000; 8,000 to 120,000; 20,000 above) - the income-tier practice ran from 2024 under RSSB administration while the gazetted instrument dates to February 2026, a validation-year grounding gap recorded as a parity finding rather than back-dated in the encoding.
metadata.title: Official Gazette no Special of 23/02/2026 - Prime Minister's Order No 001/03 of 16/02/2026 (CBHI funding; repeals Order No 105/03 of 30/09/2020) and Ministerial Order No 002/26/10/TC of 16/02/2026 (member premiums by IMIBEREHO registry level)

metadata.publisher: Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda (no 44 of 31/10/2016); Ministry of Public Service and Labour document library
metadata.source_authority: Republic of Rwanda (Minister of Public Service and Labour)
metadata.source_note: Ministry of Public Service and Labour library print of the Official Gazette no 44 of 31/10/2016 carrying Ministerial Order No 007/16/10/TC of 28/10/2016 related to the maternity leave benefits scheme (96-page text-embedded trilingual gazette issue), issued under Law No 003/2016. Grounds the chapter on the components of the gross salary to which the 0.3 + 0.3 percent maternity contribution is subscribed - the contribution base RWAMOD RW_2025 operationalizes as gross employment income.
metadata.title: Ministerial Order No 007/16/10/TC of 28/10/2016 related to the maternity leave benefits scheme - components of the gross salary to which the contribution is subscribed
